content: Hiya folks, I'd love to get some reviews on https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250317-cstr-core-v9-0-51d6cc522f62@gmail.com/ since this is the kind of refactor that gets harder to land as Rust code is added to more trees.

I opened this ACP in response to your thread when we last discussed it:
https://github.com/rust-lang/libs-team/issues/550

But I guess it has stalled.
I also opened https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/138498, which similarly stalled
This seems like an impasse. Should we pivot to a wrapper around core::ffi::CStr that implements Display? With a From impl it could be relatively ergonomic: c"hello world".into().

If upstream is still deciding the CStr thin pointer thing, then we should just wait. If they are not deciding and thus they don't plan to change anything soon, do we need to use core::ffi::CStr to begin with?
Are there any advantages other than c"" literals? I'm not sure there are...
No, of course we don't. The benefits are c"hello" literals, reduced duplication, and alignment with upstream. The longer we wait, the less are our chances of influencing them.
What I'd like to understand is what we're waiting on. Surely we don't care about the thin pointer thing directly - so what is it? The Display impl? What else?

Right now, the series is a trade-off between:

  • You gain the c"" syntax.
  • You lose impl Display.

I'm not convinced this trade-off is worth it. If we get impl Display, then that concern goes away, of course.
Great, so is that the only concern? Would it be addressed by the wrapper I suggested?

The longer we wait, the less are our chances of influencing them.

I am not sure I follow.
Well, to the extent that there are unstable APIs we lose the ability to weigh in if they're stable by the time we use this type.
But that is orthogonal -- we can talk to them with or without the series.
I don't see how us rushing to use it with a workaround influences them. In fact, it could be the opposite effect, since if the workaround is enough, then it means we could keep using it.

Yeah, of course we can talk to them either way. What I mean is that we won't discover other potential paper cuts until after we start using it.
I don't think us using it or not influences them at all, it just gives us more exposure, and thus more time, to influence them in the normal ways.

I didn't respond on those threads but I believe the sized_hierarchy RFC is likely to be accepted at some point and paves the way for a thin CStr. IMO you should just open a PR that uses BStr's implementation to provide Display which libs-api can FCP. That can be orthogonal from a Deref impl to sidestep the thin question
E.g. String has a deref to str but still has its own Display

@Trevor Gross you're saying I should add Display to CStr?

That's https://github.com/rust-lang/libs-team/issues/550, right? I guess there hasn't been any movement there.
Yeah, sometimes "just do it" to get things in an votable state gets more attention than a discussion post. As long as it's not too much effort that you'd be sad about losing in case it gets declined.
Makes sense. Andrew Gallant engaged with the ACP, and I created a tracking issue. https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/139984
I’m not really sure why that was suggested, there isn’t much point in a tracking issue for things that are instantly stable :)
Ah right, new process in case whoever puts up the implementation isn’t the same person to author the ACP (wonder who suggested that one…)
FCP is managed on the tracking issue though, no?

For things that are insta-stable like traits, usually there isn't a tracking issue so it happens on the PR. I think there's a mild preference for FCPs on the PRs anyway now, so it's clear what exactly is being stabilized
The issue pretty much contains the implementation. I will send a PR in a bit.
Sent: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/139994.
@Trevor Gross can you clarify what you mean about doc comments? Doc comments on trait implementations don't show anywhere. Or did you mean on CStr itself?
I think they show up
E.g. https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/primitive.char.html#impl-Pattern-for-char
TIL
In any case, something somewhere to indicate what we are guaranteeing with the PR (so figuring out what bstr does isn't required first)
